title: trifolium montanum subsp. montanum
intro: Trifolium montanum subsp. montanum, also known as alpine clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions in Europe, with a history of use in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Alpine clover is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
